summary: Backend-only first-pass testing for Myrtle and Ray site. All 38 pytest tests passed against public REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL. Verified health, public catalog (13 characters, 13 products, 7 download categories, 17+ downloads, pages, activities), public submissions (contact, wholesale, mailing-list dedupe, download-capture), JWT auth (cookies + Bearer + 401/403 gating), admin CRUD for products/characters/downloads/categories/pages/settings/mailing-list, email outbox queueing while RESEND_API_KEY is empty, and media upload/list/delete with file accessibility under /uploads.

minor.issue: PUT performs $set with the full provided content object, so partial updates wipe untouched keys. Acceptable for current usage but consider deep-merge or whitelist.
If body is empty, still runs $set with only updated_at — no 400 returned. Minor consistency gap vs character PUT which raises 400 on empty.
Re-queues a brand-new pending row instead of flipping the existing one — could leave duplicate pending items in the outbox.

allow_origins=['*'] combined with allow_credentials=True is invalid per CORS spec; browsers will refuse credentialed requests from a real origin. Use the explicit production origin instead of wildcard.
Cookies are set with secure=True, samesite='none'; this works on HTTPS but blocks local http testing. Fine for the preview domain.

action_items: Optional: tighten CORS — replace allow_origins=['*'] with explicit production origin since allow_credentials=True is set.
Optional: have /api/admin/pages do a deep-merge of content to avoid wiping fields on partial PUT.
Optional: /api/admin/email-outbox/{id}/retry should update the original row's status instead of creating a new queued row.
critical_code_review_comments: auth.py _set_cookies uses secure=True samesite='none' — correct for prod HTTPS, but be aware it disables localhost http testing.
public_router.py /downloads/{slug}/track does not return 404 when slug missing (just no-op update). Consider verifying existence.
admin_router.py upload accepts pdf/zip/svg with no content sniffing — relies on extension; OK for MVP but a security hardening item.
server.py CORS misconfigured: '*' + allow_credentials=True is non-compliant.
admin_router.py page PUT replaces the whole content dict on $set, which can erase fields if the UI sends partial content.
test_credentials: community@rollingriver.com / Camp1993! (verified working). httpOnly cookies + Bearer fallback both verified.
seed_data_creation: Tests create TEST_-prefixed products, downloads, categories, mailing-list subscribers, and a 1x1 PNG media item. Each is cleaned up within its own test (create→get→delete). Submissions (contact/wholesale/download_capture) and a few outbox rows remain in DB and can be left as-is; they are clearly TEST_-prefixed.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend P0 is fully green. Re-run via `pytest /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py -v`. RESEND_API_KEY is intentionally empty — email_outbox rows stay status=pending. Note: TestAdminPages.test_update_homepage_hero overwrites homepage_hero.content with {'headline': 'TEST_marker_*'} and does not restore — main agent or next test should re-seed if hero copy is needed; seeding is idempotent only on first insert so a manual restore may be required. TestAdminSettings.test_update_settings restores tagline back to default at end of test.
rca of the issue: No bugs reproduced; all P0 endpoints behave per spec.
